title: ficus costigera
intro: Ficus costigera, also known as the mangrove ficus, is a tropical tree species native to the coastal regions of Asia, specifically in the mangrove forests of Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ficus costigera include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Ficus costigera are commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
